repo.name: ripgrep
setup: No dependency setup is required for read-only benchmark inspection.

prompt: Explain how ripgrep parses CLI flags, walks candidate files, and executes a search over each haystack through matcher, searcher, and printer components. Cite the source files that support the path.

expected_symbols.name: main
expected_symbols.why: CLI entry point that delegates into the search driver.

expected_symbols.name: run
expected_symbols.why: Selects search, files, types, or generate modes after flag parsing.

expected_symbols.name: search
expected_symbols.why: Single-threaded directory walk and per-file search loop.

expected_symbols.name: SearchWorker
expected_symbols.why: Coordinates matcher, searcher, and printer for each haystack.

expected_symbols.name: SearchWorker::search
expected_symbols.why: Executes one haystack search including preprocessors and decompression.

expected_claims.text: main calls run after flags::parse and routes into search or parallel search modes.
HiArgs builds walkers, matchers, searchers, and printers used by the search driver.
search walks haystacks from the ignore crate and invokes SearchWorker per file.
SearchWorker connects a PatternMatcher, grep searcher, and Printer for each haystack.
search_parallel uses walk_builder().build_parallel() to search files concurrently.

forbidden_claims.text: ripgrep searches files without building haystacks from the directory walker.
Flag parsing happens inside SearchWorker instead of before run selects a mode.

notes: Generalization holdout for retrieval retrieval. Do not tune planner or ranker heuristics against this manifest.
